outliers.title: How India Quietly Killed the REAL Diamond (And You'd NEVER Know)

transcript_analysis.transcript_preview: Kind: captions Language: en In January of 2024, a six karat diamond reached the International Gemological Institute in Tel Aviv. Flawless to the eye and the loop, it carried a laser inscription matching a real grading report for a natural. And at that size, it would have sold as natural for well over $100,000. A masterpiece.
